title: modes::BehaviorMode serde_default missing on modifiers field breaks backwards compat
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
kiss
## Location
src/modes/mod.rs:251-269 (BehaviorMode struct)
## Violation
```rust
pub struct BehaviorMode {
    pub agency: Agency,
    pub quality: Quality,
    pub scope: Scope,
    pub modifiers: Vec<Modifier>,
}
```

The struct test `serde_missing_modifiers_defaults_to_empty` (line 809) explicitly documents this is a known regression: sessions saved before the `modifiers` field existed fail to deserialize. The test passes by asserting on the failure, but that's a deferred fix masquerading as a passing test.

```rust
assert!(
    result.is_err(),
    "BehaviorMode currently requires modifiers field; \
     add #[serde(default)] to make it optional"
);
```

The fix is one line and the comment explicitly states it. Every session resume after a deploy that adds new fields will fail in the same shape unless the team commits to a default-everywhere policy.
## Mandated refactor
Add `#[serde(default)]` to every field of `BehaviorMode` (and to the Agency/Quality/Scope variants once they have a `Default` impl — they do). Convert the negative-assertion test into a positive assertion verifying the field defaults to an empty vec. Establish a project-wide rule: any added enum/field on a persisted struct gets `#[serde(default)]` so wire-format additions are non-breaking.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
